summary: axiosqqq typosquats axios and silently pulls unrelated transitive dependencies
details: Package name `axiosqqq` is a 3-character suffix typosquat of the popular `axios` HTTP client. The tarball replicates axios's source verbatim — README, CHANGELOG, MIGRATION_GUIDE, dist/lib bundles, TypeScript definitions, repository URL (`https://github.com/axios/axios.git`), author (`Matt Zabriskie`), and homepage (`https://axios-http.com`) — so a developer who mistypes `axios` as `axiosqqq` gets a working drop-in and is unlikely to notice. However, `package.json` declares two dependencies that real axios does not: `@caspianph/storyteller@^1.0.0` (an unrelated scope) and `math@^0.0.3` (a known squat-target package on npm). Neither dep is referenced anywhere in the package's own source — they exist solely to be transitively installed into the victim's environment when `npm install axiosqqq` runs. Whatever code those packages publish (now or in the future) will be pulled into the installer's dependency tree under the guise of an axios install. The metadata impersonation (upstream repo, author, homepage) confirms deliberate deception rather than an accidental fork.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
